reasoning: The forecast window to achieve and publicly announce AGI is only ~7.5 months (before Jan 1, 2026). While there is definitely massive hype around advanced capabilities in 2025 (with OpenAI’s o3 model passing ARC-AGI and initially some claims from Sam Altman about 2025), Altman has since clarified in a Jan 2025 interview that his previous 2025 target was just a "random number" set 10 years ago. Other leading labs definitively place their timelines further out — Anthropic expects major leaps around 2026-2027 but refuses to use the "AGI" label, and DeepMind’s leadership (Demis Hassabis) and recent 145-page paper explicitly forecast true AGI closer to 2030. Furthermore, OpenAI is severely legally and financially disincentivized from officially "announcing" AGI right now, as recent late 2024 reports show their Microsoft contract binds them to a newly formed financial definition of AGI requiring the system to generate at least $100 billion in profits for its earliest investors—a milestone they are currently no where near. With the major labs actively pushing realistic timelines to 2027-2030+ and severe structural hurdles to any early formal announcement, the likelihood of a legitimate, declared AGI before 2026 is extremely low. I estimate 2% (the floor).
